name: Ari Atoll

name: Tulamben

name: Bunaken

name: Malapascua

name: Apo Reef

name: Trincomalee

name: Gulf of Tadjoura

name: Mnemba Atoll

name: Watamu Marine Park

name: Tofo

name: Memba Bay
description: Unstudied northern Mozambique coastline with seasonal whale shark and manta ray sightings, 1,000 km north of the well-known Tofo aggregation.

name: Aliwal Shoal
description: Main ragged tooth shark season and a favored cool water offshore window.

name: Sodwana Bay

name: Mahé

name: Praslin

name: Mitsamiouli

name: Sal

name: São Tomé North Coast

name: Cozumel

name: Riviera Maya Cenotes

name: Silfra Fissure

name: Great Blue Hole

name: Turneffe Atoll
description: Spring through early summer is a common favorite for weather and visibility.

name: Utila

name: Roatán

name: Cocos Island

name: Catalina Islands
description: Dry season Pacific diving often brings easier surface conditions and mantas.

name: Coiba National Park
description: Main access season with calmer crossings and productive Pacific diving.

name: Bocas del Toro
description: One of the calmer Caribbean windows before stronger rainy stretches.

name: Malpelo Island

name: Providencia

name: Wolf Island

name: Arraial do Cabo

name: Los Roques

name: Bonaire National Marine Park
description: Famous year round shore diving destination with consistently accessible reefs.

name: Westpunt
description: Leeward Caribbean diving with broad year round reliability.

name: Tiger Beach

name: Bloody Bay Wall
description: Classic wall diving destination with broad year round reliability.

name: Saba Marine Park
description: Favored season outside the hurricane period for pinnacles and seamounts.

name: St. Eustatius Marine Park
description: Best weather usually falls outside the late summer storm season.

name: Bianca C Wreck
description: Dry season months are commonly preferred for wreck and reef combinations.

name: The Pit
description: Offshore conditions tend to be strongest in spring to early summer.

name: Poor Knights Islands
description: Late summer through autumn is a favored window for warmer water and visibility.

name: Milford Sound
description: Summer is the most practical season for access and weather in Fiordland.

name: Ningaloo Reef

name: Julian Rocks

name: Kimbe Bay
description: The drier season is typically preferred for reefs, wide angle and easier travel.

name: Milne Bay
description: Long broad season for muck, reef and liveaboard itineraries.

name: Beqa Lagoon
description: Dry season shark and reef diving is widely favored in Fiji.

name: Great White Wall
description: Soft coral wall diving is often especially good in the cooler dry season months.

name: Blue Corner

name: Rock Islands
description: Dry season conditions usually make inter island boat travel easier.

name: Manta Ridge
description: Main manta season with calmer trade wind conditions.

name: Chuuk Lagoon
description: Main wreck diving season with more settled weather.

name: SS President Coolidge
description: Dry season wreck diving window with clearer water and easier access.

name: Tiputa Pass
description: Long main season for pass diving, dolphins and big schools.

name: South Pass
description: Favored shark and spawning season for many advanced pass diving trips.

name: Bonegi Wrecks
description: Dry season visibility and logistics are generally strongest in this period.

name: Niue Reef
description: Winter dry season months are often preferred for visibility and cave entries.

name: Medes Islands
description: Warm season Mediterranean diving with marine reserve visibility and life.

name: La Restinga
description: Long Atlantic island season with stable weather and volcanic topography.

name: Vis Island
description: Best warm water Adriatic season for caves, walls and wrecks.

name: Gozo
description: Main season for archways, caverns and comfortable Mediterranean water temps.

name: Santa Maria
description: Warmest and most reliable offshore season for mobulas and blue water diving.

name: Capo Carbonara
description: Warm season Mediterranean visibility and reef life are best in summer and early fall.

name: Kornati National Park
description: Summer is the main boat diving season through the Kornati islands.

name: Florida Keys
description: Warm water reef season before winter cold fronts become more common.

name: Kona Coast
description: Warm season clear water diving with manta and lava topography highlights.

name: Channel Islands
description: Late summer and early fall usually bring the calmest conditions and best visibility.

name: Jardines de la Reina
description: Main liveaboard window outside the peak storm season.

name: Silver Bank
description: Peak humpback interaction season in the North Atlantic.

name: Anse Chastanet
description: Drier Caribbean season generally offers the easiest boat and shore conditions.

name: Speyside
description: Favored season for Tobago drift diving before wetter summer conditions.

name: Larnaca
description: Warm Mediterranean wreck season with calmest seas and best visibility from late spring through autumn.

name: Salt Island
description: Dry season Caribbean window for the BVI's protected wreck and reef diving outside the late summer hurricane months.

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: The world's largest coral reef system, stretching over 2,300km off Queensland. Back-to-back mass bleaching events in 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, and 2024 have transformed large sections. Diving here documents what survives and where recovery is taking hold.

name: Malmok
description: Calm leeward bay off northwest Aruba sheltered from the trades, home to the WWII Antilla wreck and several smaller artificial reefs accessible by boat.

name: Scapa Flow
description: A wide, sheltered anchorage in Orkney, Scotland, holding the scuttled remains of the German High Seas Fleet from 1919 — the world's largest collection of divable WWI warships.

name: Tela
description: Coral cover above 60 percent when the Caribbean average has fallen below 20. Sea urchin populations here survived a 1983 pathogen that wiped out 95 percent of them everywhere else in the region. Scientists from the University of Miami, Operation Wallacea, and the Coral Reef Alliance are actively studying why — and early DNA work in 2024 found an unusually heat tolerant symbiotic algae inside Tela's elkhorn corals that is extremely rare elsewhere. Researchers are now crossbreeding Tela's elkhorn corals with Florida's nearly extinct populations to carry that resilience to reefs far beyond Honduras.

name: Wakatobi
description: A UNESCO Biosphere Reserve at the heart of the Coral Triangle, Wakatobi is one of Indonesia's most intact reef systems. Operation Wallacea has run continuous scientific monitoring here since 1998 and documented steady coral recovery following a 2010 bleaching event. Hard coral cover held at nearly 30 percent park-wide in 2019 surveys while destructive fishing declined. In 2024 when 84 percent of the world's reefs experienced bleaching-level heat stress, Wakatobi showed no significant bleaching — an anomaly researchers attribute to locally evolved heat tolerance in the coral colonies.

name: Sogod Bay
description: Sogod Bay hosts the second largest whale shark aggregation in Asia, with LAMAVE researchers having identified more than 350 individual sharks over a decade of monitoring. The outer reefs around Panaon Island reached 60 to 70 percent live coral cover in 2020 Oceana surveys — roughly three times the Philippine national average. Panaon Island was formally designated a Protected Seascape under national law in August 2025. Few dive operators work here and crowds are minimal even during peak whale shark season from November through April.

name: Derawan Archipelago
description: Derawan is one of the only places on Earth where reef manta rays are resident year-round. The Marine Megafauna Foundation has identified 155 individual mantas here — and 24 percent of the females have been observed pregnant, the only site in East Borneo where this is documented. With 500 plus coral species and 800 plus reef fish species the archipelago sits in the highest-diversity marine zone on the planet. The 285,549 hectare Berau MPA was declared in 2016 and active enforcement patrols with radar monitoring began in 2023.
